description:
You are an on–call operations engineer who has just received an alert about a spike of
    “ERROR” / “CRITICAL” events for 12-June-2024.  
    A colleague has already copied last night’s raw logs into  

        /home/user/incident_logs  

    The directory now contains three plain-text files:

        /home/user/incident_logs/app1.log
        /home/user/incident_logs/app2.log
        /home/user/incident_logs/system.log

    1. Inspect the three log files and extract **only** the lines that
       a) belong to 12-June-2024 and  
       b) are classified as either “ERROR” or “CRITICAL”.
       • In app1.log and app2.log the date stamp is written “2024-06-12”.  
       • In system.log the date stamp is written “Jun 12”.  
       • Severity keywords appear literally as “ERROR” or “CRITICAL”
         (all upper-case) somewhere on the same line.
       Use a single, case-sensitive regular expression (or a pipeline of commands that
       rely on regex filters) to collect the matching lines from the three
       different files.

    2. Write all matched lines—without any duplicates—into  

           /home/user/incident_logs/critical_2024-06-12.log

       Output format requirements for critical_2024-06-12.log
       • one full, unaltered log line per row;  
       • preserve the original line order **as it appears chronologically in each
         source file** (i.e. the first relevant line from app1.log, followed by the
         second relevant line from app1.log, … then the first relevant line from
         app2.log, etc.);  
       • terminate the file with a single trailing newline character.

    3. Create a very small CSV summary file called  

           /home/user/incident_logs/summary.csv

       The CSV must have **exactly two columns**: “filename” and “total_errors”.
       Count how many lines from *each* source file made it into
       critical_2024-06-12.log and list the totals.  
       Output format requirements for summary.csv
       • The very first line must be the header  
             filename,total_errors  
       • Every subsequent line must contain the original log file name and the
         corresponding integer count, comma-separated.  
       • Lines must appear in the order: app1.log, app2.log, system.log.  
       • End the file with a single trailing newline character.

    4. Double-check that:
       • /home/user/incident_logs/critical_2024-06-12.log contains
         only the five requested lines (nothing more, nothing less);  
       • /home/user/incident_logs/summary.csv matches the exact layout described
         above and that the counts are correct.

    When you have finished, the automated test suite will:
       • open /home/user/incident_logs/critical_2024-06-12.log and compare it
         byte-for-byte to the expected content;  
       • open /home/user/incident_logs/summary.csv and do the same.

    You do not need root access for any step.
truth:
Initial directory tree (already present before the agent starts):

    /home/user/incident_logs/
        app1.log
        app2.log
        system.log

    Exact initial file contents:

    -----  /home/user/incident_logs/app1.log  -----
    2024-06-12 08:55:23,123 - INFO - Service started
    2024-06-12 09:01:02,456 - ERROR - Failed to connect to database
    2024-06-12 09:05:10,890 - WARN - Retrying connection
    2024-06-12 13:15:45,321 - CRITICAL - Out of memory
    2024-06-13 10:01:57,777 - ERROR - Timeout while processing request
    ------------------------------------------------

    -----  /home/user/incident_logs/app2.log  -----
    [2024-06-11 23:59:59] DEBUG Initializing
    [2024-06-12 00:00:01] ERROR Disk full
    [2024-06-12 08:00:00] INFO Job started
    [2024-06-12 12:30:00] CRITICAL Kernel panic
    [2024-06-12 17:45:10] INFO Job finished
    [2024-06-13 00:01:01] ERROR Disk almost full
    ------------------------------------------------

    -----  /home/user/incident_logs/system.log  -----
    Jun 12 00:05:01 localhost CRON[1234]: (root) CMD (backup.sh)
    Jun 12 01:10:45 localhost kernel: [123456.789] ERROR CPU overheating
    Jun 12 02:22:11 localhost kernel: [123557.890] INFO CPU temperature normalized
    Jun 13 04:01:33 localhost kernel: [123999.123] CRITICAL Kernel crash
    ------------------------------------------------

    Expected new file #1 (must be created by agent):

    /home/user/incident_logs/critical_2024-06-12.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2024-06-12 09:01:02,456 - ERROR - Failed to connect to database
    2024-06-12 13:15:45,321 - CRITICAL - Out of memory
    [2024-06-12 00:00:01] ERROR Disk full
    [2024-06-12 12:30:00] CRITICAL Kernel panic
    Jun 12 01:10:45 localhost kernel: [123456.789] ERROR CPU overheating
    ------------------------------------------------
    (Note: exactly 5 lines, final newline after the last line.)

    Expected new file #2 (must be created by agent):

    /home/user/incident_logs/summary.csv
    ------------------------------------------------
    filename,total_errors
    app1.log,2
    app2.log,2
    system.log,1
    ------------------------------------------------
    (Note: 4 lines total including header, final newline after the last line.)
name: task_000000_c86a34d4
